---
title: "Analisis Lab 3"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r libraries}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r smt}
load("../Data/Lab3/listings.RData")
df <- listings
head(df)
```

```{r another}
cat("Número de filas:   ", nrow(listings), "\n")
cat("Número de columnas:", ncol(listings), "\n\n")
```

```{r another one}
# Tipos de variables
cat("Tipos de variables:\n")
str(listings)
```

```{r yet another one}
# Explicación general
cat("\n── Resumen general ──\n")
summary(listings)
```
===Analisis de media y demas===


```{r graphs start}
listings$price_num <- as.numeric(gsub("[$,]", "", listings$price))

# Variables de interés
vars <- c("price_num", "bedrooms", "accommodates", "bathrooms",
          "beds", "review_scores_rating", "reviews_per_month",
          "availability_365", "minimum_nights")
df <- listings[, vars]
```

```{r statistic description}
# ── 1. ESTADÍSTICAS DESCRIPTIVAS ─────────────────────────────────────────────
summary(df)

# Estadísticas manuales por variable
for (v in vars) {
  cat("\n──", v, "──\n")
  x <- df[[v]][!is.na(df[[v]])]
  cat("  Media:    ", round(mean(x), 2), "\n")
  cat("  Mediana:  ", round(median(x), 2), "\n")
  cat("  SD:       ", round(sd(x), 2), "\n")
  cat("  Min/Max:  ", round(min(x), 2), "/", round(max(x), 2), "\n")
  cat("  Q1/Q3:    ", round(quantile(x, 0.25), 2), "/", round(quantile(x, 0.75), 2), "\n")
}
```

```{r claude cooking}
# ── Valores faltantes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
na_pct <- sort(sapply(df, function(x) round(mean(is.na(x)) * 100, 2)), decreasing = TRUE)
na_pct <- na_pct[na_pct > 0]

par(mar = c(4, 12, 3, 2))
barplot(rev(na_pct), horiz = TRUE, las = 1,
        col  = "#E05C5C", border = "white",
        xlab = "% valores faltantes",
        main = "Variables con valores faltantes")

```

```{r price distribution}
# ── Distribución de precio (sin outliers) ─────────────────────────────────────
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 2))
precio_filtrado <- df$price_num[df$price_num < 500 & !is.na(df$price_num)]

hist(precio_filtrado, breaks = 60,
     col = "#5B8DB8", border = "white",
     main = "Distribución de precio por noche (< $500)",
     xlab = "Precio (USD)", ylab = "Frecuencia")
abline(v = 193, col = "#E05C5C", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend("topright", legend = "Mediana: $193", col = "#E05C5C", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

```

```{r boxplot for price, bedrooms y accomodates}
# ── Boxplot precio ~ bedrooms y accommodates ──────────────────────────────────
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 4, 4, 1))

df_filt <- df[df$price_num < 800 & !is.na(df$price_num), ]

boxplot(price_num ~ bedrooms, data = df_filt[!is.na(df_filt$bedrooms), ],
        col = "#5B8DB8", border = "gray30", outline = FALSE,
        main = "Precio por habitaciones\nr = 0.4766",
        xlab = "Nº habitaciones", ylab = "Precio/noche (USD)")

df_filt$acc_grp <- cut(df_filt$accommodates,
                        breaks = c(0,2,4,6,8,Inf),
                        labels = c("1-2","3-4","5-6","7-8","9+"))
boxplot(price_num ~ acc_grp, data = df_filt[!is.na(df_filt$acc_grp), ],
        col = "#6DBF8E", border = "gray30", outline = FALSE,
        main = "Precio por capacidad\nr = 0.5345",
        xlab = "Huéspedes", ylab = "Precio/noche (USD)")

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

```

```{r rating distribution}
# ── Distribución de ratings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
par(mar = c(4, 4, 3, 2))
hist(df$review_scores_rating[!is.na(df$review_scores_rating)],
     breaks = 40, col = "#E8A838", border = "white",
     main = "Distribución de puntuación general",
     xlab = "Review score rating", ylab = "Frecuencia")
abline(v = 4.78, col = "#E05C5C", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend("topleft", legend = "Media: 4.78", col = "#E05C5C", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
```


```{r analisis}
# ==================== ANÁLISIS CON GGPLOT2 ====================

# 1) Convertir price a numérico (ya lo hicimos como price_num, pero lo confirmamos)
listings$price <- as.numeric(gsub("[$,]", "", listings$price))

# 2) Verificaciones rápidas
summary(listings$price)
class(listings$price)

set.seed(123)
sample_listings <- listings %>% sample_n(5000)

sample_listings2 <- sample_listings %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(price),
    !is.na(bedrooms),
    !is.na(accommodates),
    !is.na(room_type),
    price < 1000
  )

# CORRELACIONES
cor_bedrooms <- cor(sample_listings2$price, sample_listings2$bedrooms, use = "complete.obs")
cor_accommodates <- cor(sample_listings2$price, sample_listings2$accommodates, use = "complete.obs")

print(paste("Correlación price vs bedrooms:", round(cor_bedrooms, 4)))
print(paste("Correlación price vs accommodates:", round(cor_accommodates, 4)))

# SCATTER PLOTS
ggplot(sample_listings2, aes(x = bedrooms, y = price)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Relación entre número de habitaciones y precio",
    x = "bedrooms",
    y = "price"
  )

ggplot(sample_listings2, aes(x = accommodates, y = price)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Relación entre capacidad y precio",
    x = "accommodates",
    y = "price"
  )

# COMPARACIÓN
ggplot(sample_listings2, aes(x = room_type, y = price)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Precio según tipo de habitación",
    x = "room_type",
    y = "price"
  )

# Resúmenes estadísticos
summary(sample_listings2[, c("price", "bedrooms", "accommodates")])
table(sample_listings2$room_type)

```

```{r pre procesamiento}
# Seleccionar y limpiar variables relevantes para predecir price
model_df <- listings %>%
  mutate(
    # Target: price limpio
    price = as.numeric(gsub("[$,]", "", price)),
    
    # Variables categoricas -> numericas
    is_superhost     = ifelse(host_is_superhost == "t", 1, 0),
    is_instant_book  = ifelse(instant_bookable == "t", 1, 0),
    
    # Rates de texto -> numerico
    host_response_rate_num   = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", host_response_rate)),
    host_acceptance_rate_num = as.numeric(gsub("%", "", host_acceptance_rate)),
    
    # room_type como factor
    room_type = as.factor(room_type)
  ) %>%
  select(
    price,
    bedrooms, accommodates, bathrooms, beds,
    review_scores_rating, reviews_per_month,
    availability_365, minimum_nights,
    is_superhost, is_instant_book,
    host_response_rate_num, host_acceptance_rate_num,
    room_type
  ) %>%
  # Eliminar filas con NA en target o features clave
  filter(
    !is.na(price), price > 0, price < 1000,
    !is.na(bedrooms),
    !is.na(bathrooms),
    !is.na(beds)
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), median(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

cat("Dimensiones del dataset limpio:", nrow(model_df), "filas x", ncol(model_df), "columnas\n")
summary(model_df)
```

```{r caracteristicas}
model_df <- model_df %>%
  mutate(
    # Ratio precio por huesped
    price_per_guest = price / accommodates,
    
    # Ratio camas por habitacion
    beds_per_bedroom = ifelse(bedrooms > 0, beds / bedrooms, beds),
    
    # Categoría de tamaño del alojamiento
    size_category = case_when(
      accommodates <= 2  ~ "small",
      accommodates <= 5  ~ "medium",
      accommodates <= 8  ~ "large",
      TRUE               ~ "extra_large"
    ) %>% as.factor(),
    
    # Alojamiento bien valorado (rating >= 4.8)
    high_rated = ifelse(!is.na(review_scores_rating) & review_scores_rating >= 4.8, 1, 0),
    
    # Alta disponibilidad (más de la mitad del año libre)
    high_availability = ifelse(availability_365 > 182, 1, 0)
  )

cat("Features finales:", ncol(model_df), "\n")
cat("Nuevas features creadas: price_per_guest, beds_per_bedroom,",
    "size_category, high_rated, high_availability\n")

```

```{r entrenamiento 70/30}
set.seed(42)

n        <- nrow(model_df)
train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.70 * n))

train_df <- model_df[ train_idx, ]
test_df  <- model_df[-train_idx, ]

cat("Total de observaciones: ", n, "\n")
cat("Train: ", nrow(train_df), "filas (", round(nrow(train_df)/n*100), "%)\n")
cat("Test:  ", nrow(test_df),  "filas (", round(nrow(test_df)/n*100),  "%)\n")

# Verificar distribución del target en ambos sets
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
hist(train_df$price, breaks = 40, col = "#5B8DB8", border = "white",
     main = "Price — Train", xlab = "Precio (USD)")
abline(v = median(train_df$price), col = "#E05C5C", lwd = 2, lty = 2)

hist(test_df$price, breaks = 40, col = "#6DBF8E", border = "white",
     main = "Price — Test", xlab = "Precio (USD)")
abline(v = median(test_df$price), col = "#E05C5C", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

cat("\nMediana price Train:", median(train_df$price), "\n")
cat("Mediana price Test: ", median(test_df$price),  "\n")
```
